steps.title: Identify the angle relationship from the figure
steps.content: Line $s$ is a transversal cutting parallel lines $q$ and $r$. The figure marks a $61^\circ$ angle at line $q$ and angle $y^\circ$ at line $r$. These are co-interior angles (same-side interior angles), which are supplementary when lines are parallel.

steps.title: Solve for x and test all choices
steps.content:
Substituting $y = 119$ into $y = 2x + 9$:
$$119 = 2x + 9 \implies 2x = 110 \implies x = 55$$
Now verify each choice by back-substituting into $y = 2x + 9$ and checking $61 + y = 180$:
  • Choice A — $x = 26$: $y = 2(26)+9 = 61$. Then $61 + 61 = 122 eq 180$. ✗
  • Choice B — $x = 35$: $y = 2(35)+9 = 79$. Then $61 + 79 = 140 eq 180$. ✗
  • Choice C — $x = 55$: $y = 2(55)+9 = 119$. Then $61 + 119 = 180$. ✓
  • Choice D — $x = 65$: $y = 2(65)+9 = 139$. Then $61 + 139 = 200 eq 180$. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
